payload.input: I will have every boy in that youth group questioned. I will—"  "It’s not a prank," Maya sobs, crawling forward to grasp his pant leg, pressing her forehead against his knee in a parody of repentance. "It’s a mistake. I’m sorry. I’ll never see him again. Please don’t look in my room. Please don’t look in the drawer."  It is the wrong thing to say. Her father’s eyes narrow, sharp and suspicious, and he turns back toward the stairs, the photograph clutched in his fist. "The drawer?" he repeats. Maya’s blood turns to ice. She has just marked the exact location of the phone, the crypto wallet, the evidence of their true sin. She opens her mouth to correct herself, to say she meant the closet, the bed, anywhere else, but her father is already mounting the stairs two at a time, driven now by a new suspicion, a new target for his wrath.
